501(c)(3) Nonprofit · Gaylord, Michigan

Gaylord Firefighters' Association

Supporting the brave men and women of the Otsego County Fire Department — through safety, training, and community unity.

🖤 Support Our Crew Our Story →
Who We Are

Rooted in Gaylord,
built on service

The Gaylord Firefighters' Association has been a cornerstone of northern Michigan for generations. We are a dedicated group of men and women who answer the call — not for recognition, but because this community is our home.

Beyond emergency response, we work to educate, support, and strengthen the bonds that make Gaylord the close-knit community it is. From school visits to neighborhood fundraisers, we're here every step of the way.

Our association supports active firefighters, retirees, and their families — because those who serve deserve to be taken care of, too.

70+
Years of Service
40+
Active Members
500+
Calls Per Year
100%
Community Funded
Our Mission

Safety. Training.
Community.

"Dedicated to promoting safety, training, fostering community through education, collaboration and unity by supporting the firefighters of the Otsego County Fire Department."
🛡️

Safety & Training

We promote safety and invest in rigorous training so Otsego County firefighters are always prepared to protect our community.

🎓

Education

Through outreach and education programs, we help our community understand fire prevention and emergency preparedness.

🤝

Collaboration & Unity

We foster a spirit of brotherhood and community — supporting our firefighters, their families, and the people of Gaylord together.

Make a Difference

Support your
firefighters

Your donation funds critical equipment, training programs, and support for the brave men and women of the Otsego County Fire Department — and their families.

Donations may be tax-deductible. We are a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

Every Dollar Makes a Difference

Your contribution goes directly toward equipment, training, and support for the firefighters of the Otsego County Fire Department and their families.

Tap the button to donate through Venmo · @GaylordFirefighters

May 2026

Forcible Entry Fundraiser

Hands-on training open to all skill levels. Students practice forcible entry techniques across multiple stations. Limited to 20 firefighters.

$125 Registration Per Firefighter · All proceeds benefit GFA's Training Facility Project

Register Now →

Our beloved community tradition — all proceeds go toward new training equipment. Everyone welcome.
